generated_oml:
// C1: Models/Data - Enriched BIM Models
instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "The DT's semantic backbone incorporating static geometry, topology, and functional attributes (e.g., device placements, control logic) via model-driven transformations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineering
]

// C2: Models/Data - Runtime Data Pipeline
instance RuntimeDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Centralized repository for operational metrics (e.g., energy consumption, occupancy) fed by sensor networks and middleware"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelToModelTransformations, ModelToTextTransformations
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensors tracking building occupancy levels for occupancy forecasting"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors measuring real-time energy consumption metrics for energy optimization analytics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

instance VisualComfortSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors measuring visual comfort parameters (e.g., lighting levels) for adaptive lighting optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VisualComfortDataTransmitted
]

instance NonVisualHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors measuring non-visual health effects (e.g., air quality) for holistic building performance monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HealthDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data Transmissions
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance VisualComfortDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VisualComfortSensor
]

instance HealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom NonVisualHealthSensor
]

// C5: Data Types
instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operational metrics representing building occupancy levels for predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EnergyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption metrics for energy optimization and benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance VisualComfortData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Visual comfort parameters (lighting levels, glare metrics) for adaptive lighting control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VisualComfortDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveLightingService
]

instance HealthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Non-visual health metrics (air quality, CO2 levels) for holistic building performance"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HealthMonitoringService
]

// C6: Enablers/Tools
instance ModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework automating extraction of domain semantics from BIM and generating runtime artifacts (service interfaces, deployment scripts)"
    DTDFVocab:enables DTDeploymentService
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange middleware enabling bidirectional communication between physical and virtual building assets"
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rich visualization environment for interactive DT monitoring and simulation"
]

instance BIMTools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM tools (Revit, ArchiCAD) for device modeling and control logic definition"
]

instance ReinforcementLearningBOREALIS : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning framework for adaptive control policies (e.g., energy-aware lighting optimization)"
]

// C7: Model Transformations
instance ModelToModelTransformations : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated transformations converting BIM models into runtime interfaces and deployment artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables DTDeploymentService
]

instance ModelToTextTransformations : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generating deployment artifacts (scripts, configuration files) from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentAutomationService
]

// C8: Physical Acting Components
instance SmartLightingActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Integrated smart lighting systems (TwinLight project) with bidirectional control logic for adaptive lighting"
    DTDFVocab:interactsWith VisualComfortDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance AdaptiveLightingController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Adaptive lighting systems optimized via BOREALIS reinforcement learning for energy efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:controls SmartLightingActuator
]

instance HVACControlActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Building control devices for HVAC systems with bidirectional interaction via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:interactsWith EnergyDataTransmitted
]

// C9: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time visualization of building states (lighting levels, HVAC performance) via Unreal Engine"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing building operations through energy efficiency improvements using reinforcement learning"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyBenchmarkingInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service delivering predictive analytics for life-cycle management (occupancy forecasting, performance benchmarking)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ForecastingInsight
]

instance AdaptiveLightingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing adaptive lighting control policies via BOREALIS reinforcement learning"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComfortOptimizationAction
]

instance HealthMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service tracking non-visual health metrics for holistic building performance"
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualityInsight
]

// C10: Insights/Actions
instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D interactive visualization of building states (lighting levels, HVAC performance) for immersive monitoring"
]

instance EnergyBenchmarkingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis of energy consumption against baseline/LEED standards"
]

instance ForecastingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for occupancy forecasting and equipment usage patterns"
]

instance ComfortOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of lighting parameters to optimize occupant comfort levels"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AirQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of indoor air quality parameters (CO2, VOCs) for health optimization"
]

instance CostSavingsReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated report generating cost savings estimates from energy optimization efforts"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance WellnessMetricsReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generated report summarizing non-visual health metrics and wellness impact assessments"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C11: Environment Structure
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Academic building environment containing core components"
    base:contains EnrichedBIMModel, SmartLightingActuator,
                 HVACControlActuator, OccupancySensor,
                 EnergyConsumptionSensor, VisualComfortSensor,
                 NonVisualHealthSensor, ReinforcementLearningBOREALIS
]

instance LEEDPlatinumLivingLab : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "High-performance academic building with LEED Platinum certification as the DT deployment environment"
    base:contains AcademicBuildingEnvironment, UnrealEngine5,
                 ModelDrivenEngineering, EclipseHono
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals and commands generated by the DT are sent back to physical building systems via the same middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) to automate adjustments in real-time. For example, the DT can dynamically adjust lighting or HVAC systems based on reinforcement learning policies (as demonstrated in the **BOREALIS** project) or user-defined control strategies embedded in BIM."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates aligned to operational building cycles (e.g., sensor data refresh rates, control loop frequencies). The framework supports **low-latency bidirectional communication** (e.g., <100ms for critical control signals) to ensure seamless alignment between physical and virtual states."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is a **service-oriented, modular architecture** with:
  - A **centralized DT framework** (hosted on standardized extension points) that orchestrates real-time data exchange (via Hono/Ditto) and service integration.
  - **Decentralized services** (e.g., lighting control, energy optimization) deployed as pluggable modules, enabling scalability and future extensibility.
  - **BIM as the semantic glue**, ensuring consistent interpretation of physical and virtual data across the DT ecosystem."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the entire life cycle of the building, covering:
  - **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models with device configurations and control logic (e.g., lighting automation).
  - **Construction phase**: Validation and deployment of DT artifacts (e.g., middleware configurations for Eclipse Hono/Ditto).
  - **Operational phase**: Real-time monitoring, adaptive control (e.g., via reinforcement learning in **BOREALIS**), and continuous evolution of services (e.g., via DT Engineers). The DT represents the building as **operated**, emphasizing runtime synchronization with physical assets."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT fidelity is ensured through:
  - **Semantic alignment**: Model-driven transformations preserve BIM semantics (e.g., device relationships, control logic) to minimize semantic drift.
  - **Real-time synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto ensures low-latency data exchange, reducing discrepancies between physical and virtual states.
  - **Verification/validation**: ODTE metric evaluates DT entanglement to quantify interoperability and architectural robustness.
  - **Uncertainty handling**: Runtime data from sensors and control systems is processed to account for operational variability (e.g., in adaptive lighting via BOREALIS)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical connections are established via:
  - **Middleware protocols**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto for real-time data exchange (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) between physical sensors/actuators and DT services.
  - **Network architecture**: Scalable, service-oriented design supporting decentralized or centralized deployment topologies.
  - **BIM-to-DT integration**: Model-to-model transformations (e.g., via MDE) to map BIM attributes (e.g., device IDs, control rules) to DT runtime interfaces."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed via:
  - **Automated pipelines**: Model-driven deployment from BIM to runtime artifacts (e.g., service configurations, middleware endpoints).
  - **Standardized extension points**: Enable modular service integration without redeployment (e.g., adding new analytics or control strategies).
  - **Unreal Engine hosting**: Interactive visualization environment for end-users (facility managers, DT engineers).
  - **Cloud/edge deployment**: Middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) supports distributed hosting for scalability."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with:
  - **External BIM systems**: Standardized extension points allow seamless interaction with third-party BIM tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) for collaborative workflows.
  - **IoT platforms**: Middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) enables bidirectional communication with IoT sensors/actuators beyond the building perimeter.
  - **Analytics platforms**: Custom services can integrate with external data lakes or AI/ML tools for advanced decision support."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is governed by:
  - **Physical building owner**: Primary ownership of sensor data and operational metrics, with DT services acting as intermediaries for processing/analysis.
  - **Middleware compliance**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto adheres to privacy standards (e.g., GDPR) for data transmission and storage, ensuring secure handling of sensitive operational data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to:
  - **BIM standards**: ISO 12006-2, IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) for semantic consistency in building models.
  - **Middleware standards**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto for real-time data exchange, ensuring interoperability with IoT and OT ecosystems.
  - **Model-driven engineering (MDE)**: Industry-standard metamodels and transformations (e.g., QVT, ATL) for automated DT generation.
  - **AECO-specific standards**: Alignment with AECO workflows (e.g., TwinLight/BOREALIS projects) for domain-specific control configurations."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Encrypted data transmission**: Middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) supports TLS/SSL for secure communication between physical and virtual assets.
  - **Access controls**: Role-based permissions for DT Engineers, Facility Managers, and Building Control Engineers to prevent unauthorized modifications.
  - **Remote operation safety**: DT services are designed to execute non-hazardous commands (e.g., lighting adjustments) remotely, avoiding direct human intervention in critical systems.
  - **Fault tolerance**: Real-time synchronization mechanisms ensure resilience against data loss or latency, minimizing operational disruptions."
]
